differential_diagnoses: Infective endocarditis (Staphylococcus aureus or viridans streptococci) with septic emboli
Systemic lupus erythematosus (SLE) with secondary vasculitis
Primary angiitis of the central nervous system (PACNS)
next_steps: Perform urgent transthoracic and transesophageal echocardiography (TTE/TEE) to evaluate for valvular vegetations
Order comprehensive antiphospholipid antibody panel (Lupus anticoagulant, anticardiolipin IgG/IgM, anti-beta2 glycoprotein I)
Initiate specialized infectious disease testing including PCR and serology for Rickettsia rickettsii and Leptospira
Repeat blood cultures with prolonged incubation and include anaerobic/fungal coverage
Evaluate inflammatory markers (ESR, CRP) and complement levels (C3, C4)
Consult neurology for repeat imaging correlation and to assess for neuro-vasculitis vs. embolic phenomenon
Restrict intravenous fluids due to pulmonary edema while maintaining hemodynamic support with vasopressors if necessary
